info.title: TextCycler
info.description: Utility to cycle messages in text files, useful for OBS Streaming

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: CommandLineApplication.Parse, OK: CommandLineApplication.Execute, OK: CommandLineApplication.ExecuteAsync, OK: CommandLineApplication.ctor(), OK: CommandLineApplication.ctor(IConsole), OK: CommandLineApplication.ctor(IConsole, String), OK: CommandLineApplication.ctor(IHelpTextGenerator, IConsole, String))

options.name: --configFile
options.description: The config file to be used. To generate a blank config file, use the --generateConfig option with this option, and a blank config file will be generated in this path.

options.name: --index
options.description: Defines index of the text to be set on the output file. If not provided, TextCycler will cicle through the texts provided in the config file.

options.name: --sequenceValue
options.description: Defines an arbitrary value for a sequence. Specify the sequence index and the value, separated by a comma.

options.name: --targetFile
options.description: Overrides the 'targetFile' setting in the config file with a path to a file in which the next text will be written to.

options.name: --initialTime
options.description: Overrides the initial time for the time replacement tokens and countdowns.

options.name: --autoCycle
options.description: Automatically cycle the texts, pausing for the specified interval.

options.name: --variable
options.description: Asks for the value of a variable, which can be used in a replacement token in the format {VARIABLE_NAME}. Optionally, you can provide a default value to the variable, which will be displayed at prompt, using a colon after the variable name, and providing the value after that colon.

options.name: --variableValue
options.description: Sets the value of a variable, which can be used in a replacement token in the format {VARIABLE_NAME}. You must provide the value for the variable using a colon after the variable name, and providing the value after that colon.

options.name: --askForVariables
options.description: Detects variables in current or provided text, and asks for the values of the detected variables, which can be used in a replacement token in the format {VARIABLE_NAME}.

options.name: --delay
options.description: If defined, waits for the specified number of seconds after setting the target file. Useful for letting the result message be read.

arguments.name: DELAY TO BE WAITED FOR AFTER SETTING THE TARGET FILE TEXT, IN SECONDS

options.name: --menu
options.description: Displays a menu for selecting the desired text.
